name: Christy's Wood Oven Style Pizza

ingredients.item: 8 oz can of San Marzano tomatoes plus one can of water

method: Proof the yeast in 1 cup of warm water for 10 minutes. In a large bowl, mix flour and salt. Combine yeast mixture slowly into flour mixture, use your hands to mix. Continue mixing until the flour and water are combined and you can handle the dough. The less flour and the less you knead the dough, the harder the pizza crust will come out. I add flour and knead the dough just a little, not too much, I knead it more after the first rise. I add the yeast water very slowly until I'm able to handle the dough, but the dough should be wet.

Place dough in a bowl and cover with a dish towel. Leave to rise in a warm place for an hour. After the dough has risen to double, punch down, add more flour to handle the dough and knead for 3 minutes, this gives the dough a little stretch. If you like rustic artisan pizza, just knead it less. The more you knead the dough, the softer it is, that's what I have come to learn with this technique. It took me a long time to perfect it, and I made mistakes at first, remember practice makes perfect! Replace towel and let rise for another hour in a warm place. In the meantime, prepare pizza pans by lining them with parchment paper. I use a large 12 inch round pizza pan with holes. See my pizza recipes in this chapter for more pizza ideas.

In a frying pan on medium heat, add 1 T olive oil and an onion quarter. Add tomatoes, water, salt, pepper, and oregano if you like, and 3 torn basil leaves to the sauce. Cover and simmer on low for 15-20 minutes. When water is absorbed, remove onion and set sauce aside. Prepare cheeses by grating the parmesan and cutting the mozzarella into strips. Set aside.

When your dough has risen for an hour, punch down, and transfer to a semolina-dusted cutting board. Preheat oven to 460 °F. With a rolling pin, roll out dough to the desired thickness you want for your pizza. Margherita pizza is a little thicker with a 1 inch crust around the edge. It doesn't have to be perfect. Drizzle olive oil on the top and spread into the crust evenly. Transfer carefully onto your prepared parchment paper. Bake on the bottom rack of the oven for approximately 10 minutes, but depending on your oven, times may vary. When evenly browned on bottom, take pizza out of the oven. Add sauce and cheese toppings if making a Margherita, cook on top rack on broil (500°) for 5 minutes or until the top is brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly. If you char the crust it looks like it was baked in a wood oven. Transfer to a cutting board and slice with a pizza cutter. Garnish with basil leaves. Makes one large 12 inch pizza. Olé
